text: U-Net (Ronneberger et al. 2015) introduced a symmetric encoder-decoder with skip connections, becoming the de facto standard for biomedical image segmentation and winning multiple ISBI challenges.
Segment Anything (SAM, Kirillov et al. 2023, Meta AI) introduced a promptable foundation model for segmentation that can segment any object in any image given a point, box, or mask prompt.
Mask R-CNN (He et al. 2017, Facebook AI) extended Faster R-CNN by adding a parallel mask prediction branch, enabling instance segmentation with minimal overhead and winning the COCO challenge.
